free_text: The photoneutron cross sections of B-10 and B-11.
The targets, comprising 6.5 g/cm**2 of B-10 (99% pure
isotope),  and  8.8 g/cm**2 of B-11 (80%  B-11,  20%
B-10),  were pressed from the respective isotopes in
powder  form  to  obtain  self supporting pellets of
such a size that eclipsing geometry was ensured  for
The  cross  sections  were  obtained  from  the
experimental yields using the unfolding variable-bin
Penfold-Leiss  method.  Measurements  of  the  yield
curves  was  carried  out  using  a  constant energy
interval of 0.1 MeV between successive points and an
average curve for each target was obtained from five
individual yield curves for B-10 and from seven  for
B-11.  In  contrast  to  the situation for medium to
heavy nuclei where a statistical model can  be  used
to account for processes such as (g,2n), no suitable
model is available  for  light  nuclei  so  that  no
attempt  was  made  to  correct  the  measured yield
curves for any multiplicity of neutron counting. For
both  isotopes  the total photoneutron cross section
presented is the  sum:  sig(g,tn)  =  sig(g,n)  +
The absolute  total  photoneutron  cross  sections of
B-10 and B-11 have been deduced from  bremsstrahlung
yield  curves  for  the  reactions measured from the
threshold to 28 MeV.  Neutron yields  were  measured
using  direct  neutron  counting  and  were analysed
using the  variable-bin  Penfold-Leiss  method.  The
integrated  cross  sections  from thresholds to 27.6
